response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine systems chops, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. Professional C++ experience amounts to roughly one year (National Instruments, 2014-2015) against a hard requirement of eight; his high-performance distributed-systems work was primarily in D at Sociomantic, and his recent years are TypeScript/Node.js product work. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership track record leading platform or infrastructure teams, and cloud experience is at the user level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys) rather than building compute platforms.
strengths: Real low-latency distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Broad, long-tenured engineering career with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, test-framework authorship, teaching workshops)
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing through courses, conference talks, and long-running open-source projects
concerns: Far short of the 8-year C++ requirement — only ~1 year professional C++ plus hobby/thesis work
No production cloud-architecture or internal compute-platform experience; cloud exposure is consumer-level (AWS CDK, k8s deployment)
No formal technical-lead role owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables; recent work is full-stack product engineering

key_factors.factor: Cloud platform and technical-leadership track record
